$comment: Parity-recovery Stage 0: the migration loss report is a CHECKED ARTIFACT. Every lossy note migrateNotebookV1 emits for the live notebook.json must match one of these approved classes; a note matching none fails the suite (an unapproved loss appeared). Classes are matched by substring. Stage 4 eliminates classes one by one — remove the entry here in the same commit that restores the semantics, so the approved set shrinks with the work. Target: empty.

approved.why: v1 `when` (minHoriz etc.) approximated by traversal-graph feasibility
approved.restoredBy: Stage 4: geometric when-gates via world queries

approved.match: face: dropped — facing follows the approach direction
approved.why: v1 authored arrival/step facing overridden by approach direction
approved.restoredBy: Stage 4: arrival face restored as authored choice

approved.why: authored movement tempo flattened to the locomotion solver's feel
approved.restoredBy: Stage 4: named motion profiles preserve authored tempo

approved.match: acting pose during a move approximated
approved.why: v1 pose-during-move became an onLaunch cue (jump moves only)
approved.restoredBy: Stage 2a/4: acting layer holds the pose through the move

approved.why: v1 camera focus target / unmapped travel target coarsened
approved.restoredBy: Stage 4: camera targets + edge target refs restored

approved.why: authored zoom/tempo flattened to the follow camera
approved.restoredBy: Stage 2a: camera intent {mult, fast} wired to the shell

approved.why: v1 page effects (smoke/crack/shove) reduced to sound intents
approved.restoredBy: Stage 2c/4: fx intents drive the real overlays

approved.why: v1 edge-side targets coarsened to the roof spot
owner-authored weight 0 = deliberately out of the pool (not a loss)
